lesson: A small block sits on top of a larger block. The larger block is on a horizontal table with no friction. The top block has mass m1 = 1.8 kg, and the bottom block has mass m2 = 4.2 kg. The coefficient of static friction between the two blocks is mu_s = 0.35. A horizontal force of magnitude F is applied to the bottom block. Determine the largest value of F for which the two blocks can accelerate together without the top block sliding on the bottom block. Take g = 9.8 m/s^2 and give your answer in Newtons.
